Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Feb-11-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
George Wynns (georgewynns@gmail.com) for Plaintiff.
Glenn Broemer (805.663.2654; glenbroemer@gmail.com) for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Patrick Gavin's Motion To Tax Costs:

Defendant Patrick Gavin's motion to tax or strike costs is denied as untimely. Defendant judgment debtor challenges a memorandum of costs after judgment that was served by mail on December 28, 2020 and filed on December 31, 2020. A judgment debtor may file a motion to tax costs within 10 days after the memorandum of costs is served on the judgment debtor. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 685.070(c).) "If no motion to tax costs is made within the time provided in subdivision (c), the costs claimed in the memorandum are allowed." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 685.070(d).) Here, service of the memorandum of costs was complete on January 12, 2021 (allowing five days for service by mail under Code of Civil Procedure sections 685.070(f) and 1013), but Defendant did not file his motion to tax costs until January 15, 2021, three days too late. Accordingly, the costs claimed in the memorandum must be allowed. (See also California Practice Guide: Enforcing Judgments and Debts (The Rutter Group 2020) par. 6:51 ["If a timely motion to tax costs is not filed by the judgment debtor, enforcement costs claimed in the judgment creditor's memorandum are automatically allowed and added to the judgment"].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Plaintiffs Application Package And Request For Prove Up Hrg
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Patrick J. Gavin and John Gavin.
Dept. 514. Judge: A. James Robertson, II. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

George S. Wynns, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Nimer Massis, who was placed under oath and testified, and for plaintiff Jennifer Nushwat, who was not present at this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Patrick J. Gavin or John Gavin.
Plaintiff's complaint for Breach of Contract/Common Counts was filed on July 10, 2018, and personally served on defendant Patrick J. Gavin on July 13, 2018.
Substitute service on defendant John Gavin was effectuated on October 2, 2018.
Default as to defendant Patrick J. Gavin was entered on September 11, 2018.
Default as to defendant John Gavin was entered on December 7, 2018.
DOE defendants were dismissed on October 1, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Nimer Massis and Jennifer Nushwat, and against the defaulted defendants Patrick J. Gavin and John Gavin, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $66,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $3,764.25.
Attorney Fees - $7,500.00.
Costs - $514.00.

Total - $77,778.25.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
